habitat: Woody areas near water; mangroves; paddy fields; freshwater wetlands; and forest edges
story: An icon of the skies, the Buffy Fish Owl (Ketupa ketupu) is a species from the Strigidae family. It thrives in Woody areas near water; mangroves; paddy fields; freshwater wetlands; and forest edges, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Least Concern (LC), the Buffy Fish Owl is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the Buffy Fish Owl helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the Buffy Fish Owl, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
